priceSnapshot.note: After the midday drop through the $65,500/$65,380 shelf on the Brent>$100 oil shock, BTC stabilized and held the $65,000 handle into the US cash close (~$65,047, down ~1.56% on the day from a $66,081 open). Still inside the broader $64,000-$66,800 range; the tactical bull shelf remains lost but the range floor is intact.

reduceIf.confirmation: 1H close below $64,062 loses the range/invalidation floor — scale the remaining core toward EXIT

addIf.confirmation: 4H close back above $65,500 reclaims the lost shelf — re-add the tranche trimmed at midday

decisionBox.changed: Midday's tactical trim (fired at ~$65,380) is done; BTC then held $65,000 rather than cascading, so the remaining long-term core stays on HOLD above the $64,062 invalidation. With no report until ~06:50, a resting stop at $64,062 is set as the only overnight protection.

overnightRisk: Intel reports after the close tonight (Street ~$0.01 EPS on ~$11.91B revenue) and can swing Nasdaq futures and broad risk appetite into the Asia handover; today's Alphabet (-7.1%) and Tesla (-14.5%) post-earnings plunges already set a defensive tone. Brent near $99 after spiking above $100 on US-Iran tensions, and the US 10Y at 4.66% (highest since Jan 2025), keep risk assets pressured. No report until ~06:50, so the $64,062 resting stop is the sole protection against an overnight gap.

atr.source: Not verified this run (exchange APIs blocked from sandbox). Prior verified daily ATR(14) ~$1,553 (~2.36%) on Jul 22 — deliberately NOT carried into any calculation here.

timeframes.structure: Stabilized after the midday flush; chopping $64,900-$65,200 with buyers defending the $65,000 handle into the close.
timeframes.source: Price action per Yahoo Finance / crypto.news (Jul 23); intraday indicator table not verified (exchange APIs blocked)

timeframes.structure: Lost the $65,380 shelf earlier — that level now caps as resistance; holding the $65,000/$64,800 demand into the close keeps this a range-low base rather than a fresh leg down.
Broke the lower edge of the multi-session compression but is basing above the $64,062 range floor, so this still reads as a range-low test rather than a confirmed trend break.
The July repair pulled back from the $66,000 cap on the oil/yields risk-off; daily EMA200 still sits well above price (higher-TF downtrend intact). RSI not re-verified this run.
timeframes.source: Daily RSI not re-verified this run (morning Investing.com read was ~61.4; not carried forward)

scalpContext.note: Scalp only the edges — live signals come from the chart, not this report.

etfFlows.note: US spot BTC ETF inflow streak extended to 7 sessions; cumulative ~$999.38M since Jul 14, with the last reported session ~$68.99M (below Jul 21's $203M). Jul 23's flow prints after the US close and is not yet verifiable, but the structural bid remains the bulls' best card against the oil/yields risk-off.

liquidations.note: CoinGlass live snapshot shows BTC open interest ~$48.3B; a clean, current long/short liquidation split was not verifiable this run and is left null rather than fabricated.

earnings.note: Q2 beat (rev $96.43B, EPS $2.31) but the 2026 capex guide lifted to $195-205B spooked investors; a drag on the Nasdaq Jul 23.
Q2 rev $22.5B (-12% YoY) and EPS $0.40 both missed; a major weight on megacap/risk sentiment Jul 23.
Reports after the close tonight; Street ~$0.01 EPS on ~$11.91B revenue — an overnight risk swing for Nasdaq futures and broad appetite.

indices.note: Closed -1.90% at $691.96 (prev close $705.35, day's range $687.79-$698.66) as $100 oil and rising yields hammered tech; today is the lowest close of the trailing 21 sessions.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: Closed -1.23% at $678.61 (prev close $687.03, day's range $675.76-$682.55); broad market pulled back off the top of its monthly range on the oil/yields risk-off.

dcaSignal.note: Computed from trailing daily closes — relative-cheapness for monthly DCA, not a bottom call.

indices.note: StockAnalysis feed is STALE again — last print is dated Jun 18, 2026 ($100.24 prev close), not a current Jul 23 session. Per the data-integrity rule, price/changePct are null and dcaSignal is omitted.

calibration.rollingRecord: Range-with-bearish-tilt calls tracked well this week; the morning $65,380 reduce fired at midday and BTC then stabilized ~$65,000 rather than cascading, so the trim locked a modest edge and freed dry powder.
calibration.note: Midday flagged the morning's $65,380 reduce as FIRED and set a $64,062 invalidation. Into the close BTC held $65,000 (~$65,047) instead of extending toward the $64k floor, so the feared cascade did not materialize — trimming at ~$65,380 vs the ~$65,047 close still captured a small edge and avoided drawdown, so actingHelped = true. The long-term spot core holds above the $64,062 invalidation.
